data.title: BorgWarner recognized on TIME's 2025 World's Best Companies List in collaboration with Statista, a leading data provider.
data.description: In September 12, 2025, Auburn Hills, Michigan, BorgWarner was named to TIME's prestigious World's Best Companies 2025 list. This recognition was achieved with the help of Statista, a top global provider of market and consumer data and rankings. The award acknowledges BorgWarner's excellence in the industry.

data.title: Powerfleet (Nasdaq: AIOT) to attend Barrington Research Virtual Fall Investment Conference on Sep 16th.
data.description: Located in Woodcliff Lake, New Jersey, on September 12, 2025, Powerfleet, Inc. (Nasdaq: AIOT) shared plans to participate in the Barrington Research Virtual Fall Investment Conference on September 16th. The company's management will be present at the conference to engage with investors regarding Powerfleet's AIoT platform. This event provides an opportunity for Powerfleet to discuss its strategies and growth prospects with interested parties.

data.title: Workdry International acquires Atlas Dewatering in Florida to strengthen North American rental business.
data.description: In West Palm Beach, Florida, as of September 12, 2025, Workdry International, the parent company of leading pump rental and wastewater treatment providers including Holland Pump, Selwood, Siltbuster, and Vanderkamp, has successfully finalized the acquisition of Atlas Dewatering. This strategic move further solidifies Workdry's position in the industry, expanding its portfolio to enhance services and solutions for its customers.

data.title: LakeShore Biopharma receives delisting determination letter from Nasdaq on September 12, 2025.
data.description: On September 12, 2025, LakeShore Biopharma Co., Ltd (Nasdaq: LSB) announced its commitment to advancing vaccines and therapeutic biologics globally. The company focuses on the discovery, development, manufacturing, and distribution of innovative medical solutions.

data.title: Timeline rebrands its skincare portfolio to align with scientific advancements, focusing on holistic wellness. September 12, 2025.
data.description: In New York on September 12, 2025, Timeline, a leading company in cellular longevity and mitochondrial health, revealed a major skincare rebranding initiative. The rebrand encompasses a revamped identity and a new skincare lineup. This marks a significant transformation for the company as it moves towards a more innovative approach in the skincare sector.

data.title: Rita's and Abbott Elementary launch mango ice, offer Extra Credit Wednesdays through Oct 15 in Philly. Celebrating new season of Abbott Elementary.
data.description: To mark the return of the popular TV show Abbott Elementary on ABC, Rita's Italian Ice is introducing a special new flavor for a limited time. Additionally, they are offering Extra Credit Wednesdays until October 15th. This exciting promotion is taking place in Philadelphia and coincides with the new season of the show. Don't miss out on this opportunity to enjoy delicious Italian Ice while tuning in to the latest episodes of Abbott Elementary.

data.title: Travelex Insurance wins 2025 Virtuoso Award for Best Specialty or Insurance Partner at Travel Week.
data.description: Travelex Insurance Services Inc. in Omaha, Nebraska, was recently awarded the 2025 Virtuoso Award for Best Specialty or Insurance Partner. This esteemed accolade was given at the 37th annual Virtuoso Travel Week from August 9 to 15 in Las Vegas.

data.title: VisitorsCoverage debuts AI Quote Assistance, revamping travel insurance selection with innovative technology.
data.description: In Santa Clara, California on September 12, 2025, VisitorsCoverage Inc. unveiled the introduction of AI Quote Assistance, making it the pioneering AI-driven quote system in the travel insurance sector. This innovative technology marks a significant advancement for the company in offering cutting-edge solutions to clients.

Oil loadings at Russia's Primorsk port were suspended earlier on Friday as Ukrainian drones hit two crude oil tankers located at the Russian Baltic Sea port.
- Two Industry Sources.

data.title: Pitney Bowes appoints Todd Everett as President of SendTech, as part of strategic review since May 2025.
data.description: In Stamford, Connecticut, Pitney Bowes Inc. (NYSE: PBI) revealed new undertakings as part of its strategic review initiated in May 2025. Todd Everett was appointed as EVP and President of Sending Technology Solutions ("SendTech"). This move comes as the company, known for its technology-driven shipping solutions, mailing services, and financial offerings worldwide, aims to enhance its operations. Mr. Everett's appointment signifies a pivotal step in the company's restructuring efforts.

data.title: TRON (TRX) jumps to $0.35 on bullish signals from technical indicators, offering new opportunities for traders.
data.description: The price of TRX has surged to $0.35, backed by a strong bullish trend indicated by various technical indicators. This uptrend is creating new trading prospects for cryptocurrency investors.

data.title: OpenAI's future governance to be decided in billion-dollar deal with Microsoft, amid concerns over massive expenses.
data.description: The AI titan is on the verge of finalizing an agreement with Microsoft regarding its forthcoming management, although concerns linger about its substantial expenses.

data.title: CUPE local 1792 members rally in Cornwall to stop planned care time cuts at Glen Stor Dun Lodge, due to significant staffing shortages.
data.description: In Cornwall, Ontario, members of CUPE local 1792 organized a rally and information picket on Montreal Road to raise awareness about the proposed reductions in care time at Glen Stor Dun Lodge, a municipally-owned long-term care facility. The union, which represents workers at the lodge, highlighted that their members have faced severe staffing shortages for an extended period. The recently announced cuts to working hours are expected to exacerbate the already challenging working conditions at the publicly managed Long-Term Care facility. The protest aimed to draw attention to this issue and urge authorities to address the situation promptly.

data.title: Huize Holding's Q2 EPS increased to $0.02 from a loss of $0.20 last year, with sales rising to $55.38 million from $38.94 million. First-year premiums jumped to 1.127 billion yuan ($158.316 million), and gross written premiums increased to 1.796 billion yuan. CEO aims to revolutionize insurance value chain with AI technology.
data.description: Shares of Huize Holding Limited (NASDAQ: HUIZ) are seeing an increase in premarket trading on Friday following the company's strong second-quarter performance. Their adjusted earnings per share for the quarter were two cents, a significant improvement from a loss of 20 cents in the same period last year. Quarterly sales also saw a substantial growth, reaching $55.38 million compared to $38.94 million in the previous year. Huize recorded a 73.1% increase in first-year premiums, totaling 1.127 billion Chinese yuan ($158.316 million), while gross written premiums rose by 34.4% to 1.796 billion yuan. This positive trend reflects the company's robust performance in the online insurance product and service sector.
